phase_note: Phase 1 uses manifests to force every future semantic slice to carry explicit upstream error precedents, not only success-path examples.

cases.upstream_focus: Negative tests should assert structured parser failures with source locations instead of vague generic exceptions.
cases.zr_decision: ZR reference alignment treats negative coverage as mandatory first-class work, starting with parser and compiler failures.

cases.upstream_focus: EOF diagnostics should stay specific enough to distinguish literal termination problems from other parse failures.
cases.zr_decision: ZR keeps an explicit unclosed-string fixture rather than hiding it behind broader parser smoke tests.

cases.upstream_focus: Assignment and malformed-expression diagnostics need fine-grained error categories, not one catch-all message.
cases.zr_decision: ZR compiler tests should keep target-specific failures such as const reassignment isolated in dedicated fixtures.

cases.upstream_focus: Bad argument forms deserve intentional recovery tests so diagnostics remain actionable at the boundary between parsing and semantic checks.
cases.zr_decision: ZR will grow additional negative call fixtures from this manifest instead of treating named-argument misuse as unspecified behavior.

cases.upstream_focus: Import diagnostics need scope-aware failures when a referenced member exists but is not legally accessible.
cases.zr_decision: ZR import follow-up work should distinguish not-found, duplicate, and inaccessible module/member cases.

cases.upstream_focus: Immutable-write failures should be compiler diagnostics with a stable category, not a runtime side effect.
cases.zr_decision: ZR phase 1 adds a compile-fail const-reassignment fixture as the first concrete immutability diagnostic baseline.
